manifest.name: Rust Test Intent & Coverage Map
manifest.description: Catalogs the intent and scope of the Rust test suite. This manifest tracks test_role, tested_behavior, risk_covered, and target_components to enable agents to quickly locate relevant tests, understand coverage gaps, and identify regression risks.

analyzers.name: Rust Test Coverage Intent
analyzers.description: Analyzes Rust files to extract test coverage intent, identifying test roles, frameworks, behaviors, and risks with strict validation.

fields.name: has_test
fields.description: `true` if this file contains executable tests, test macros, assertions, fuzz targets, benchmark validation, or test harness logic. `false` for pure fixtures even if used by tests, and for non‑test files.

fields.name: test_frameworks
fields.description: Rust test mechanisms or project conventions used, e.g., `["cargo-test", "cfg-test", "rust-test-attr", "rgtest-macro", "proptest", "quickcheck", "compiletest", "cargo-fuzz", "criterion", "custom-harness"]`. **Always an array**, never a string.

fields.name: tested_behavior
fields.description: 3‑10 behavior‑level labels. Prefer action‑object names: `"detect-binary-file"`, `"handle-nul-byte"`, etc. **Always an array**; empty if not applicable.

fields.name: tested_flags_or_api
fields.description: Exact CLI flags, public APIs, trait methods, config options, or macros tested. Preserve exact spelling, e.g., `"--binary"`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: target_components
fields.description: Implementation areas under test, e.g., `["cli", "searcher"]`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: platform_scope
fields.description: `["all"]`, or explicit platform constraints such as `["unix", "windows"]`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: fixtures
fields.description: Referenced fixture files, `include_bytes!`/`include_str!` paths, generated test files, golden output, etc. **Always an array**.

fields.name: risk_covered
fields.description: Failure modes protected by this file, e.g., `["binary-detection-regression"]`. **Always an array**.

fields.name: canonical_for
fields.description: Behaviors for which this is likely the best first file to inspect or extend. **Always an array**.

fields.name: add_test_guidance
fields.description: One concise sentence explaining when a developer should add or update tests here. For non‑test files, use the standard guidance: `"Do not add tests here; use the relevant test module for this component."`.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: For test‑related files, start with `"Use this file to test..."` or `"Use this file as..."`, and mention the primary behavior/component covered. For non‑test files, use exactly the string `"This file does not contain test coverage intent."`.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Must be `"high"`, `"medium"`, or `"low"`. Assign based on presence of direct test syntax vs. inference vs. weak evidence.

fields.name: file_extension
fields.description: The file extension without the leading dot (e.g., `js`, `md`), or `null` if none.

fields.F12: This file does not contain test coverage intent.
